title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1 / 1.2)
statement_text: For every graph $H$ there exists $c>0$ such that every $H$-free graph $G$ has a stable set or clique of size at least $|G|^{c}$.
context_text: Erdős and Hajnal posed this conjecture in 1977; it remains open for most graphs $H$. The paper focuses on the case when $H$ is a path: the conjecture had been proved only for paths on at most five vertices, and the present paper establishes a near-optimal version for all paths.
paper_contribution: Proves that for every path $P$, every $P$-free graph on $n$ vertices contains a clique or stable set of size at least $2^{(\log n)^{1-o(1)}}$, establishing the 'near Erdős-Hajnal' property for all paths and strictly improving the previously known $2^{c\sqrt{\log n \log\log n}}$ bound in this case.
notes: The conjecture appears twice: as the named 'Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture' in environment 1.1 and restated in terms of $f_H(n)$ in environment 1.2. Both refer to the same conjecture; attribution is to [6] (1977) and [7] (1989).
paper_title: Induced subgraph density. V. All paths approach Erdos-Hajnal
